pages.key_finding: User stickiness continued its upward trajectory, reaching 14.3% in March. However, the overarching trend shows a severe contraction in audience size, with Monthly Active Users (MAU) nearly halving from 37 to just 21. While the 87.7% registration conversion remains robust, the 55.4% pre-gameplay drop-off must be addressed immediately to halt the bleeding of acquired users.

pages.title: Game Performance (Score)
pages.key_finding: Following February's steep drop, game performance continued to plummet in March. The average daily score fell by another 57.7%, resting at just 12,265. This persistent downward slide strongly indicates that the remaining core players are suffering from severe burnout or lack of fresh challenges.

pages.title: Game Performance (Time)
pages.key_finding: Playtime metrics directly mirror the plummeting scores, with average daily session times being sliced in half (down 53.3% to ~15 minutes). The game is failing to hold player attention, signaling that core gameplay loops may have grown stale for the remaining audience.

pages.title: Most-played stages | Most-cooked menus
pages.key_finding: User preferences remain steadfastly anchored in the 'Umami Daily' stage and 'ข้าวผัดหมู' (Pork Fried Rice). Given the sharp decline in overall engagement, launching an event or campaign centered specifically around these proven fan-favorites could be the most effective short-term re-engagement strategy.

metrics.Top Menu: ข้าวผัดหมู
